explanation: HCF and LCM in Real Numbers focuses on connecting prime factorization and algorithmic reasoning to highest common factor and least common multiple. A Class 10 student should use this concept to compute hcf and lcm using theorem-based number reasoning.
key_points: Core idea: HCF and LCM.|Use-case: Compute HCF and LCM using theorem-based number reasoning.|Chapter context: Real Numbers.

explanation: Trigonometric Ratios in Introduction to Trigonometry focuses on defining sine, cosine, tangent and related ratios for an acute angle in a right-angled triangle. A Class 10 student should use this concept to compute and interpret trigonometric ratios from triangle sides.
key_points: Core idea: Trigonometric Ratios.|Use-case: Compute and interpret trigonometric ratios from triangle sides.|Chapter context: Introduction to Trigonometry.

explanation: Classical Probability in Probability focuses on using favorable outcomes over total equally likely outcomes to compute simple probabilities. A Class 10 student should use this concept to solve introductory probability problems using the classical definition.
key_points: Core idea: Classical Probability.|Use-case: Solve introductory probability problems using the classical definition.|Chapter context: Probability.
